scope: Galaxy of Intrigue feats only. This audit separates implementation presence from implementation correctness, and explicitly rechecks Skill Challenge feats after the Phase 3.5 subsystem work.
generatedFrom: Phase 8 feat catalog metadata, Phase 3 Galaxy of Intrigue parity manifest, current abilityMeta implementation markers, and source-derived benefit summaries.
rules: A feat is not implemented correctly just because it has metadata.
Action-option, reaction, and replacement feats must expose timing/target/resource behavior rather than flat static bonuses.
Skill Challenge feats are correct only if catalog metadata and runtime SkillChallengeFeatHooks agree.
Table-positioning feats may be metadata/partial when map-state, line-of-sight, or ally trigger automation is not proven.
Source collisions must remain source_review_required until book attribution is resolved.

entries.name: Adaptable Talent
entries.description: Prerequisites: None Effect: Choose one Talent that you meet the prerequisites for, from a Class you possess. Once per day, after at least 6 hours of rest, you can choose to swap out one of your current Talents for the Talent you chose for this Feat. The Talent you swap out cannot be the prerequisite for any other Talent you have. You can swap back to your original Talent after at least 6 hours of rest. Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Adaptable_Talent

expected.accuracyQuestion: Does the runtime implementation behave as temporary_choice_swap, instead of only providing generic metadata or a flat passive modifier?

accuracy.rationale: Persistent talent swap needs a selected talent and rest timing. Current metadata captures choice-required behavior, but no complete rest-gated swap workflow is proven.
accuracy.correctiveAction: Add targeted runtime tests and/or implementation glue before marking implemented_correct.

entries.name: Bone Crusher
entries.description: Prerequisites: Crush , Pin Effect: When you deal damage to a Grappled opponent, the opponent also moves -1 step along the Condition Track . Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Bone_Crusher

expected.accuracyQuestion: Does the runtime implementation behave as condition_track_hook, instead of only providing generic metadata or a flat passive modifier?

accuracy.rationale: Correct shape is a damage-to-grappled-target rider. Current metadata records a CT rider, but runtime listener/target-state verification is still required.
accuracy.correctiveAction: Add targeted runtime tests and/or implementation glue before marking implemented_correct.

entries.name: Brilliant Defense

accuracy.correctiveAction: Add targeted runtime tests and/or implementation glue before marking implemented_correct.

entries.name: Channel Rage
entries.description: Prerequisites: Rage Species Trait Effect: Once per day, instead of entering into a Rage, you gain a +5 bonus to your Will Defense until the end of the encounter. This bonus counts as using your Rage ability for that day. Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Channel_Rage

expected.accuracyQuestion: Does the runtime implementation behave as rage_mode_toggle, instead of only providing generic metadata or a flat passive modifier?

accuracy.rationale: Correct shape is alternate Rage use granting Will bonus. Metadata exists, but rage-state activation/consumption is not proven.
accuracy.correctiveAction: Add targeted runtime tests and/or implementation glue before marking implemented_correct.

entries.name: Cut the Red Tape
entries.description: Prerequisites: Trained in Knowledge (Bureaucracy) Effect: You can use your Knowledge (Bureaucracy) modifier instead of your Gather Information modifier when making Gather Information checks. If you are entitled to a Gather Information check reroll, you can reroll your Knowledge (Bureaucracy) check instead (subject to the same circumstances and limitations). You are considered Trained in the Gather Information skill for the purposes of this check. Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Cut_the_Red_Tape

expected.accuracyQuestion: Does the runtime implementation behave as skill_substitution, instead of only providing generic metadata or a flat passive modifier?

accuracy.rationale: Implementation metadata correctly models Knowledge (bureaucracy) substitution for Gather Information contexts, including reroll inheritance.
accuracy.correctiveAction: No corrective action required beyond regression coverage.

entries.name: Demoralizing Strike

accuracy.correctiveAction: Add targeted runtime tests and/or implementation glue before marking implemented_correct.

entries.name: Disturbing Presence
entries.description: Prerequisites: Trained in Deception Effect: If you succeed on a DC 15 Deception check, you can move through the Threatened area or Fighting Space of an enemy as part of your move action without provoking an attack of opportunity. Each Threatened space or occupied space that you move through in this manner counts as 2 squares of movement. Normal: You can Tumble through an occupied enemy square only by using the Acrobatics Skill. Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Disturbing_Presence

expected.accuracyQuestion: Does the runtime implementation behave as skill_movement_option, instead of only providing generic metadata or a flat passive modifier?

accuracy.rationale: Correct shape is a Deception check to move through threatened/occupied spaces without AoO. Metadata exists but map/path validation is table-context heavy.
accuracy.correctiveAction: Add targeted runtime tests and/or implementation glue before marking implemented_correct.

entries.name: Resilient Talent
entries.description: Once per day, you may gain the benefit of any talent from a class you have levels in for 1 minute. You may use this talent even if you do not meet its prerequisites.

expected.accuracyQuestion: Does the runtime implementation behave as temporary_talent_choice, instead of only providing generic metadata or a flat passive modifier?

accuracy.rationale: This is correctly not static sheet math. It requires a temporary talent choice and prerequisite bypass timing; keep as GM/picker metadata until a talent-temporary-state subsystem exists.
accuracy.correctiveAction: Keep excluded from static math; add dedicated picker/subsystem only if later requested.

entries.name: Desperate Gambit
entries.description: Prerequisites: None Effect: Once per turn, when you miss on an attack roll, you reroll the attack, but you take a -2 penalty to Reflex Defense until the end of your next turn. If your first attack missed because you rolled a Natural 1, you can still use this Feat, but you take a -5 penalty to Reflex Defense instead. You must accept the result of the second roll, even if it's worse than the first. Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Desperate_Gambit

expected.home: attack roll reroll / defense penalty workflow
expected.accuracyQuestion: Does the runtime implementation behave as reroll_hook, instead of only providing generic metadata or a flat passive modifier?

accuracy.rationale: Correct shape is once-per-turn miss reroll with Reflex penalty. Metadata says implemented, but resource timing and defense penalty duration require tests.
accuracy.correctiveAction: Add targeted runtime tests and/or implementation glue before marking implemented_correct.

entries.name: Grazing Shot
entries.description: Prerequisites: Point-Blank Shot Effect: When you successfully make a ranged attack against a single target, you can make a second attack roll against an additional target that is in direct line of sight, and no farther than 6 squares from the original target. If the second attack roll succeeds, make a single damage roll, and divide the damage equally between the two targets. If the second attack roll fails, you deal no damage to either target. Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Grazing_Shot

expected.accuracyQuestion: Does the runtime implementation behave as attack_option, instead of only providing generic metadata or a flat passive modifier?

accuracy.rationale: Correct shape is a second attack against a nearby additional target after a successful ranged attack. Metadata exists; target selection and LoS/range validation are not proven.
accuracy.correctiveAction: Add targeted runtime tests and/or implementation glue before marking implemented_correct.

entries.name: Hobbling Strike
entries.description: Prerequisites: Sneak Attack or Rapid Shot or Rapid Strike . Effect: Whenever you would deal extra damage by using Sneak Attack , Rapid Shot , or Rapid Strike , you can reduce the target's Speed by 1 square until the end of the encounter, instead of dealing the extra damage. Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Hobbling_Strike

expected.accuracyQuestion: Does the runtime implementation behave as attack_rider_choice, instead of only providing generic metadata or a flat passive modifier?

accuracy.rationale: Correct shape lets user trade extra damage from Sneak Attack/Rapid Shot/Rapid Strike for speed reduction. Metadata exists; chooser/toggle and duration tracking need proof.
accuracy.correctiveAction: Add targeted runtime tests and/or implementation glue before marking implemented_correct.

Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Meat_Shield

entries.name: Recurring Success
entries.description: Prerequisites: None Effect: When you select this Feat, choose one Talent or Feat that can normally be used once per encounter. You can use that Talent or Feat one additional time per encounter. Special: You can select this Feat multiple times. Each time you do so, you must choose a different Talent or Feat to gain the benefits of this Feat. Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Recurring_Success

expected.accuracyQuestion: Does the runtime implementation behave as persistent_choice_resource, instead of only providing generic metadata or a flat passive modifier?

accuracy.rationale: Correct shape requires choosing a once-per-encounter talent/feat and increasing its per-encounter uses. Needs source verification plus integration with per-encounter resource tracking.
accuracy.correctiveAction: Consult Galaxy of Intrigue/source collision context before moving to implementation.

entries.name: Resolute Stance

Normal: When you Fight Defensively you only get a dodge bonus to your Reflex Defense . Source URL: https://swse.fandom.com/wiki/Resolute_Stance
